# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF MENTAL HEALTH: The Cure For Overthinking*

- id: 30094455
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/30094455

## videos

- `iframe` https://dialog.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://www.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/

- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F30094455&title=4.%20The%20Existential%20Crisis%20Checklist%20%7C%20The%20Mystery%20School&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    4\. The Existential Crisis Checklist 

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/3YBH61i6SINna8HpuaK0_ifeelbad.JPG)

And here you are again.

In this familiar place: Your reccuring cycle of doubt.

Who put you in this state this time?

What happened in your life to cause this?

_Trick question._

You know it has nothing to do with "them" or the outside world.

How you see the world is up to you.

And you're seeing it in a negative way right now.

Could it be because you...

You feel like you've stopped growing?

An artist that's not growing feels lackluster. 

Like somethings off.

Like they're not living to their full potential.

And it's sneaky. It creeps up on you.

You may not notice it at first. Especially if you've been blending in with the "normals" who make a habit out of living like this no problem.

But for an artist... "Phoning it in" kills us.

The reason you feel bad / lost / uncentered isn't because of one bad thing that happened, or because you woke up on the wrong side of bed today...

The feelings you have right now are the result of an accumulation of little things that you stopped doing.

Somewhere along the way, maybe you got cocky.

You started believing the Hallmark Cards.

And you stopped doing the things that brought you to your power in the first place.

Despite the fact that me and everyone else around you knows you're amazing... YOU totally forgot.

And why?

Because youu stopped doing the things that reinforce your gifts. 

And now you feel "lesser than". 

From now on... The moment you feel bad, let it trigger a part of your brain that asks:

_"What did I stop doing that allowed this feeling to creep up on me again?"_

Here is a checklist of questions for you to come back to every time you feel like it's all going to shit:

  


# **1\. Are you doing your daily practice? Are you CREATING every day?**

# **2\. Are you staying off social media and other mind-numbing distractions? Especially in the morning. Especially at night.**

#  **3.**[**Are you journaling?**](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/30093395)**(This is too basic. It's crazy.) Have you read back the last few weeks to see if your current angst isn't all in your head? Are you falling in any noticeable pattern?**

#  **4\. Are you suffering from**[**"Complication Creep"**](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/23650999)**? Is it time to simplify your goals back to their essence?**

# **5\. Did you write a note to yourself when you were feeling good -- so that you could re-read it in times like these, to remind yourself what your priorities are?**

# **6\. Have you created an environment for yourself with multiple cues to remind you of your goals? Have you "refreshed them" so that they don't blend into the background (and the recesses of your mind).**

# **7\. Are you hanging out with positive, vibrant people?**

# **8\. Are you stretching, breathing, working out, meditating?**

# **9\. Are you getting enough sleep?**

  


If you aren't doing these things (the energy builders), why would you expect to be feeling good right now?

You're an artist. You need to re-align with artist-on-the-rise behaviors.

If you don't feel like immediately correcting the things you're missing above ask yourself:

**Do I love the angst?**

**Do I love the drama?**

**Do I love the emotional distress?**

**Does it make me feel "alive"?**

**If so... Am I using it at least to create something incredible?**

The fact that you're reading this is promising.

Live up to that promise with what you decide to do next.

_Your audience is waiting for you to get back to the mission._

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/30094455/comments/23977189)

i think i was in a slump for about 90 days, came back from a creative trip was doing some songwriting in jamaica and i got back home and was just BUMMED out felt like i couldnt do anything at all SLEPT on the COUCH dragged myself to the GYM did my best to eat but nothing worked. When I kinda got back on track I hurt my back and was down again, but something changed i started listening to the podcast again and i started adding healthier habits to my lifestyle also I NEVER STOPPED creating even in the bad times. but i also started to create songs for my self and Im working my way back out of the rut

My eyes grew a little misty in this one, to be honest. My days have been a cycle of this (unnecessary) existential crisis, which I know can get better...if I let it. Cause I swear; I am over the anxiety, the sadness, the fear, the cycle. Y'know? I need a new set of things to worry about. everything in this module is gonna be a game-changer for sure. Thank you

![Pixie ](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/43eb684f805e9de257a508501cad715b?d=mm&s=70)

Pixie 

Awaiting Review 

5 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/30094455/comments/13274903)

I heard a guy say recently "If you're histerical, it's historical". You know when you lose your shit because you keep encountering that same trigger. A person triggers that old wound, then it's you that decided how much is going to unravel after that. Sure they did that trigger thing, but you decided how you would react. I love that question: what did I stop doing that allowed this to creep up on me? What is on my mind right now is when you stop allowing yourself to use that trigger to self sabotage everything else and then blame that person. When historically you are the common denominator. You stop seeing that lesson when you start reacting differently. And yes I love this checklist because often its like, is my blood sugar low ? 🤣❤

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

haha... oh god yes, "is my blood sugar low". 😂💯💯 When you carry these questions around with you in your mind until THEY are the kneejerk reaction instead of the old reaction... you can cruise control for longer, not forever (who wants THAT) but longer... "is my blood sugar low?" 😂 i'm dead

Holy mcMoly. Thank you fam. I find my fulltime job my biggest energy sucker. It sucks. It makes me fucking depressed. And it is busy + hectic + exhausting. But THESE^ should not fall off. I find these ^^ fall off during times where the job gets more depressing or frustrating. Those are the times I should double down on the EC checklist.

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/30094455/comments/13213143)

This resonates! ^^^ My day job is as a creative director at an ad agency. It's the biggest energy and time suck. It keeps me from writing and directing which is my jam. I go thru spurts of getting up at 4 and getting some writing done, but I can't keep up that momentum. It gets depressing and I feel like I keep falling behind. But what I'm learning through experimentation is that it takes even more energy to get past this. We can do it.

When you can get up at 4 to do YOU before working for THEM, your moods will regulate. BUT, the problem isn't waking up at 4... It's getting to bed at the right time to make 4am feel like the breath of fresh air it COULD feel like.

![RT Herwig](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/254f48f87ea53593b3ab8b035c17b8e6?d=mm&s=70)

RT Herwig

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/30094455/comments/11215403)

This. Right. Here. Is. The. Reason. "...it's sneaky. It creeps up on you. You may not notice it at first. Especially if you've been blending in with the normals who make a habit out of living like this no problem." & "Phoning it in kills us." Yet we do it for the positions and the salary. We are lulled to sleep through a fantasy. It stops today.

That was 4 months ago. Since then I've been up and down battling this hardcore. Our ego is a tricky thing. It wants us to be logical and easy, not what's beneficial and harder. I'm trying. I'm trying.
